summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'One-proportion z-test'?

"One-proportion z-test" means: Tests H₀: p = p₀ using z = (p̂ - p₀)/√(p₀(1-p₀)/n) and the standard normal distribution. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Test statistic'?

"Test statistic" means: z = (p̂ - p₀)/√(p₀(1-p₀)/n); measures how many standard errors the sample proportion falls from the hypothesized value. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'One-sided vs. two-sided test'?

"One-sided vs. two-sided test" means: One-sided: Hₐ: p > p₀ or p < p₀ (p-value is area in one tail); Two-sided: Hₐ: p ≠ p₀ (p-value is area in both tails). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Conditions for z-test'?

"Conditions for z-test" means: Random sample or random assignment, n < 10% of population, np₀ ≥ 10 and n(1-p₀) ≥ 10. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Conclusion in context'?

"Conclusion in context" means: State the decision (reject/fail to reject H₀) and interpret in the real-world context of the problem, including the p-value. The other options describe different concepts.
